title: React Performance Optimization Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of profiling, code splitting with React.lazy and Suspense, and render prevention in React.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the first step when you suspect a performance bottleneck in your React component tree?
options: Record an interaction using the Profiler tab in the React DevTools.
Rewrite every component using class-based syntax.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of 'Code Splitting' in a large React application?
options: It makes the code easier to read.
It splits the code into smaller chunks that can be downloaded only when needed, reducing the initial bundle size.
It allows using HTML inside JavaScript.
It automatically fixes bugs in the code.

questions.question: Which React feature should you use to load a component lazily and display a fallback while it downloads?
What happens if you use React.memo() incorrectly for very small and simple components?
options: The overhead of props comparison may actually be slower than simply re-rendering a small component.
It will cause a memory leak.

questions.question: React Profiler only works in which browser development tool?
